steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim is that the authors describe how restorative sleep can be for young people. The correct quotation must mention both (1) sleep and (2) its restorative effect on the young.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Let us, dear reader remember the punishment of idle curiosity...": About curiosity and Blue Beard — no mention of sleep. ✗
  • Choice B — "Then let no one turn with too much [fussiness] from the simple story of humble Elleanor...": About the story itself, not sleep. ✗
  • Choice C — "Blessed are the slumbers of the innocent! They are kindlier than balm, and they refresh and gladden the spirit of childhood...": "Slumbers" = sleep; "refresh and gladden the spirit of childhood" = restorative for the young. Both elements present. ✓
  • Choice D — "Home is home, to the lowly as well as the great...": About home, not sleep. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
